cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user is asking which conclusion can be drawn from the history experts' statements regarding the "Happy Birthday" song. First, I'll review the passage to find where the experts are mentioned. The relevant part says: "A number of history experts say that there is no record of who actually wrote the 'Happy Birthday to You' lyrics. Historians also say there is no way to know when the general public began singing the 'Happy Birthday' song, but they believe it was being sung by the public long before it was printed and owned by a company." The question is asking what the experts' statements prove. Let's look at the options: A) The song does not belong to Warner Music Group. B) The song has more than 200 years' history. C) Was initially owned by another company. D) Has always been very popular. The experts mention that the song was being sung long before it was printed and owned by a company. That suggests that before Warner Music Group (since 1988)
